feed.title: SAP Community - API Management
feed.subtitle: API Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: API_CV_ATTAHMENT_SRV API is not working properly in supplier quotation in OnPremise S4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi All
I added attachment using API_CV_ATTAHMENT_SRV to supplier quotation in OnPremise  S4 HANA system and got  https status code 201. But Unable to see the attachment in Attachments tab of Fiori App.  Using below similar url in http POST method
https://xxx123.portal.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/AttachmentContentSet  

Could you help on this ?

Thanks,
Ramesh

entry.title: RecipientBusinessSystemID under Interface CONF_OUT
entry.content:
Hello!

In XML structure for Sales Order Acknowledgement with Interface CONF_OUT, how can I modify the RecipientBusinessSystemID of the data structure?

entry.title: Impacts of SAP API Policy v4/2026 on existing customer integrations
entry.content:
With the release of SAP API Policy v4/2026, SAP reinforces that only published APIs (as listed in SAP Business Accelerator Hub or official product documentation) can be consumed, and explicitly disallows the use of internal, private, or non-published APIs — regardless of whether the system is Public Cloud, Private Cloud, or accessed via SAP BTP.

Many customers have long-running integrations and custom architectures (including SAP BTP-based extensions) that technically work today but rely on internal or non-released APIs.

My questions to the community are:

  1. How is SAP positioning the impact of this policy on existing customer architectures that are currently non-compliant?
  2. Is SAP recommending a concrete transition strategy or grace period, or should customers proactively plan migration to published APIs?
  3. In practice, how should customers prioritize remediation for productive integrations that rely on non-published APIs?

I am particularly interested in real-life experiences, guidance from SAP, or best practices adopted by customers and partners since this policy was updated.

Link to the Policy: SAP API Policy

entry.title: Best Practices for JWT Validation and JWKS Caching in SAP API Management using KVM
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I’m designing a JWT validation flow in SAP API Management for an integration project. The goal is to keep the proxy environment‑agnostic while ensuring high performance through JWKS caching. I’d appreciate feedback on the approach and any potential improvements.

Current Flow Summary:

  1. Validate the request body to ensure it is well‑formed JSON.
  2. Remove sensitive headers (e.g., Cookies) before further processing.
  3. Read the IdP base URL from an environment‑specific KVM.
  4. Normalize the URL using JavaScript for service callouts.
  5. Look up JWKS in cache.
  6. Call the IdP’s .well-known/jwks.json endpoint if the cache is empty.
  7. Cache the JWKS (TTL: 120 seconds).
  8. Verify JWT (RS256, scopes).
  9. Validate the issuer claim against the KVM value.

    Are there alternative or recommended ways to further externalize configuration (beyond KVMs) while keeping the proxy fully environment‑agnostic?

    Looking forward to your insights on optimizing this zero‑touch setup.

    Best regards,
    Somya

entry.title: SAP Settlement Management – Retrieve PDF Form via API for Lobster Integration (XInvoice/ZUGFeRD)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm facing an integration challenge in the area of SAP Settlement Management (Condition Contract Management) and hope someone has had similar experiences or can point me in the right direction.

Background:

My customer uses Lobster as their EDI/middleware solution. For standard billing, the integration is already working: Lobster retrieves the invoice PDF via the standard OData API:

 
 
GET /sap/opu/odata/SAP/API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV/GetPDF

This works smoothly and is well documented.

The Problem:

Now we need to build an analogous process for the Customer Rebate Document from Settlement Management – i.e., the document issued to the customer during bonus settlement (Customer Rebate Settlement Document / credit memo equivalent).

The goal is to also retrieve this form via an interface and pass it to Lobster, so it can be further processed and sent as an XInvoice (XRechnung) or ZUGFeRD-compliant electronic invoice.

My Research So Far:

I have searched extensively but have not found a comparable standard API:

  • On the SAP API Business Hub (api.sap.com), there does not appear to be a dedicated OData API for Settlement Management forms / Condition Contract documents
  • API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV only covers classic SD billing documents, not Settlement Management documents
  • I am not aware of any RFC-enabled function modules that cover this use case

My Questions for the Community:

  1. Is there a (perhaps lesser-known) OData or REST API in the SAP standard that allows programmatic retrieval of a Settlement Management form PDF?
  2. Would an RFC/BAPI approach be feasible?
  3. Are there alternative integration approaches, such as:
    • Direct integration via SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) / Integration Suite?
    • Output via SPAD/Spool with subsequent retrieval via web service?
    • Using Smart Forms / Adobe Forms Output Management with a downstream retrieval step?
  4. Has anyone successfully implemented this process – rebate settlement document → middleware → XInvoice/ZUGFeRD delivery – and can share their approach?

System Environment: SAP S/4HANA (On-Premise), Lobster as middleware, target format XRechnung / ZUGFeRD 2.x

Any hint is appreciated – even a confirmed "this is not possible in standard" would already be helpful and move us forward. Thanks in advance!

Best regards

entry.title: 1470000341 Fully allocate itemCode to bin locations in warehouse - InventoryGenEntry
entry.content:
Hy Experts,

I am trying to do a storno for issueForProduction by creating an inventoryGenEntry, the original document doesn't have BinAllocations, but it always shows the same error:

Item: C90501210, Qty: 0.62, Bin: 370
Item: C90501210, Qty: 0.62
Sztornó OIGN Error: 1470000341 - Fully allocate item "C90501210" to bin locations in warehouse "30"
Sztornó OIGN Error: -10

Here is my code:

if (process.Cancelled == true)

{

recordSet.DoQuery($"SELECT TOP 1 DocEntry FROM OIGN WHERE Comments like '%Sztornó-{process.SearchKey}%'");
if (recordSet.EoF)

{

recordSet.DoQuery($"SELECT TOP 1 DocEntry FROM OIGE WHERE Comments like '%{process.SearchKey}%'");
if (!recordSet.EoF)
{

string docEntryInventoryExit = recordSet.Fields.Item("DocEntry").Value.ToString();

SAPbobsCOM.Documents issueForProduction = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenExit);

int docEntryInventoryExitInt = int.Parse(docEntryInventoryExit);
bool foundInventoryExit = issueForProduction.GetByKey(docEntryInventoryExitInt);
Console.WriteLine($"DocEntry: {docEntryInventoryExitInt}, Found: {foundInventoryExit}");


SAPbobsCOM.Documents inventoryGenEntry = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry);

for (int i = 0; i < process.jobReportItems.Count; i++)
{

JobReportItemDTO items = process.jobReportItems[i];
if (items.Input == false)

continue;

SAPbobsCOM.Items item = (SAPbobsCOM.Items)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oItems);
if (item.GetByKey(items.Material))
{

DateTime StartDate;
if (DateTime.TryParse(process.StartTime, out StartDate))
{

inventoryGenEntry.DocDate = StartDate;

}
inventoryGenEntry.Comments = "dSuite azonosító: Sztornó-" + process.SearchKey + "\n" + process.Remarks;
inventoryGenEntry.DocObjectCode = SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry;
//inventoryGenEntry.DocumentsOwner = 44;
inventoryGenEntry.Reference2 = process.Employee;

int componentLine = -1;
string warehouse = "";
for (int j = 0; j < issueForProduction.Lines.Count; j++)
{

issueForProduction.Lines.SetCurrentLine(j);

if (issueForProduction.Lines.ItemCode != items.Material)

continue;

double targetQty = Math.Abs(items.Quantity); // pl 0.62
double remainingQty = targetQty;
SAPbobsCOM.Recordset rsBin = (SAPbobsCOM.Recordset)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.BoRecordset);

rsBin.DoQuery($@"
SELECT

OBTL.BinAbs,
OBTL.Quantity AS BinQty

FROM

OIGE

INNER JOIN IGE1 ON IGE1.DocEntry = OIGE.DocEntry

INNER JOIN OILM ON OILM.DocEntry = IGE1.DocEntry

AND OILM.DocLineNum = IGE1.LineNum

AND OILM.TransType = 60

INNER JOIN OBTL ON OBTL.MessageID = OILM.MessageID

INNER JOIN OBIN ON OBIN.AbsEntry = OBTL.BinAbs

WHERE

OIGE.DocEntry = {docEntryInventoryExitInt}

AND IGE1.ItemCode = '{items.Material}'
AND IGE1.LineNum = {issueForProduction.Lines.LineNum}
AND OBTL.BinAbs IS NOT NULL ");

if (rsBin.RecordCount > 0)
{

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseType = (int)SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenExit;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseEntry = docEntryInventoryExitInt;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseLine = issueForProduction.Lines.LineNum;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.WarehouseCode = issueForProduction.Lines.WarehouseCode;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = targetQty;

if (issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Count > 0)
{

double batchRemaining = targetQty;

for (int b = 0; b < issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Count; b++)
{

issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.SetCurrentLine(b);

double originalBatchQty = issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Quantity;

if (batchRemaining <= 0)
break;

double qtyToUse = Math.Min(originalBatchQty, batchRemaining);

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BatchNumbers.BatchNumber =
issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.BatchNumber;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BatchNumbers.Quantity = qtyToUse;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BatchNumbers.Add();

batchRemaining -= qtyToUse;

}

}

if (issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.Count > 0)
{

for (int s = 0; s < issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.Count; s++)
{

issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.SetCurrentLine(s);

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.SerialNumbers.InternalSerialNumber =
issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.InternalSerialNumber;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.SerialNumbers.Add();

}

}

rsBin.MoveFirst();

while (!rsBin.EoF)
{

int binEntry = (int)rsBin.Fields.Item("BinAbs").Value;
double qtyToUse = Convert.ToDouble(rsBin.Fields.Item("BinQty").Value);

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.BinAbsEntry = binEntry;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Quantity = qtyToUse;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.BaseLineNumber = 0;
if (issueForProduction.Lines.SerialNumbers.Count > 0 || issueForProduction.Lines.BatchNumbers.Count > 0)
{

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.SerialAndBatchNumbersBaseLine = 0;

}
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Add();
Console.WriteLine($"Item: {items.Material}, Qty: {qtyToUse}, Bin: {binEntry}");
rsBin.MoveNext();

}

}
else
{

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.ItemCode = items.Material;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.WarehouseCode = item.DefaultWarehouse;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = targetQty;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.AccountCode = "96930000";
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.CostingCode = "542GYÁRT";

int binEntry = 0;
SAPbobsCOM.Warehouses Defaultwarehouse = (SAPbobsCOM.Warehouses)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oWarehouses);
if (Defaultwarehouse.GetByKey(item.DefaultWarehouse))
{

binEntry = Defaultwarehouse.DefaultBin;
Console.WriteLine($"Default bin: {binEntry}");

}

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.BinAbsEntry = binEntry;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Quantity = targetQty;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BinAllocations.Add();

}
Console.WriteLine($"Item: {items.Material}, Qty: {targetQty}");
//Console.WriteLine($"{ inventoryGenEntry.GetAsXML()} ");
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Add();

break; 

}

}
else
{

Console.WriteLine("Nem található cikk a jelentéshez");

}

}


int result = inventoryGenEntry.Add();
if (result != 0)
{

Console.WriteLine($"Sztornó OIGN Error: {company.GetLastErrorDescription()}");
Console.WriteLine($"Sztornó OIGN Error: {company.GetLastErrorCode()}");

}
else
{

Console.WriteLine("Alapanyag visszavételezés sikeresen rögzítve a termelési rendeléshez!");

}

}

}

}

Tried the BinActionType too but then the IDE sent me this:
'DocumentLinesBinAllocations' does not contain a definition for 'BinActionType' and no accessible extension method 'BinActionType' accepting a first argument of type 'DocumentLinesBinAllocations' could be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Runtime APIs
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am currently working on leveraging SAP Integration Suite Runtime APIs and have been exploring available documentation and resources.

To ensure alignment with SAP-recommended approaches and avoid any deviations from best practices, I would like to request your guidance on the following areas:

  1. Official References
    • Links to authentic and up-to-date SAP documentation for Runtime APIs.
    • Any relevant API specifications or SAP Notes that cover this topic.
  2. Recommended Approach
    • Best practices for consuming Runtime APIs in enterprise scenarios.
    • Guidance on authentication strategies, error handling, and scalability considerations.
  3. Implementation Insights
    • Any sample use cases, integration patterns, or reference implementations.
    • Recommendations based on typical customer usage.
  4. Configuration Considerations
    • Key prerequisites, roles, and configuration steps to ensure correct setup.
    • Any known limitations or important considerations.
  5. Troubleshooting & Monitoring
    • Common challenges observed and suggested resolutions.
    • Recommended approach for logging, monitoring, and diagnostics.

I have already started reviewing the available materials but wanted to validate my approach against SAP's recommended standards and see if there are any additional resources I should refer to.

entry.title: Identifying integration systems details using SFAPI Audit Log
entry.content: I have noticed that there are logs generated in the SFAPI Audit Log screen in SAP SuccessFactors system. These logs are using the user id's PTP_SFAPI and IAM_SFAPI. When checked, there is very little information on which systems are connecting to SAP SuccessFactors using the SFAPI method. As SAP has deprecated the SFAPI, I need help to identify which system is connecting to SAP SuccessFactors and then move the integration to modern methods. Please suggest how I can identify which system is connecting to SAP SuccessFactors that is responsible for these logs in SFAPI Audit Log screen.

entry.title: issue while updating pricing conditions for NBIC Purchase Orders using API_PURCHASEORDER_2
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue while updating pricing conditions for NBIC Purchase Orders using API_PURCHASEORDER_2 in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Scenario:

* PO Type: NBIC
* API: API_PURCHASEORDER_2
* Entity: PurOrderItemPricingElement
* Condition Type: PMP0

I am able to successfully PATCH the pricing condition using Postman and receive HTTP 200 OK response, but the value is not actually updated in the database. After re-reading the pricing element, the old value is restored automatically.

PATCH URL:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaseorder_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurOrderItemPricingElement(
PurchaseOrder='4500000450',
PurchaseOrderItem='00010',
PricingDocument='1000000667',
PricingDocumentItem='000010',
PricingProcedureStep='060',
PricingProcedureCounter='001'
)

Payload:

{
"ConditionRateAmount": 159.000000000,
"ConditionCurrency": "AED",
"ConditionIsManuallyChanged": true
}

Response:

* HTTP 200 OK
* No API error

However, after GET:

* ConditionRateAmount still shows old value
* Pricing seems to be redetermined automatically

Observed fields from GET response:

{
"ConditionControl": "C",
"ConditionOrigin": "C",
"ConditionIsDeletable": false,
"ConditionType": "PMP0"
}

Additional findings:

1. POST on PurOrderItemPricingElement is not supported
Error:
"Creating operations are disabled for entity"

2. PATCH on PurchaseOrderItem with NetPriceAmount also returns:
"Operation is not enabled"

3. Same API update works correctly for NB Purchase Orders, but not for NBIC.

My questions:

1. Is pricing condition update intentionally restricted for NBIC Purchase Orders in Public Cloud?
2. Are PMP0/intercompany conditions protected from API persistence?
3. Is there any supported approach to update pricing for NBIC POs through API?
4. Should pricing only be updated indirectly through condition records/info records?

Any guidance or SAP documentation reference would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: Will deprecation of Learning Token Server change the API URLs
entry.content:
Dear Team, 

We understand from https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-release-information/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/527174fdddc64102910258133d32b2ae.html?locale=en-US that authentication need to be migrated from Learning Token Server to Platform, so this changes the authentication URL that clients need to be connecting.

Will this also impact Service URLs? For example we use https://xxxxxx.plateau.com/learning/odatav4/public/user/learningEvent/v1/recordLearningEvents to record Learning Event, will this URL also should be changed and to what. Please Clarify.

Thanks for the support in advance.

entry.title: Released API DDLS Was Deleted ATC Error in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Despite Object No Longer
entry.content:
Hello,

I am working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and I am facing compatibility ATC errors during ATC checks.

The following ATC errors are raised:

  • Released API DDLS ZDD_SD_TEST was deleted

  • Released API DDLS ZDD_SD_OPENORDERITEM was deleted

  • Released API DDLS ZDD_SD_DELIVERY was deleted

Error example:

Changes in ZSD (DEVC), compared to version 100 (CG4), found by component BC-SRV-APS-EXT-REL:
Released API DDLS ZDD_SD_TEST was deleted.

Background:

  • These CDS views were previously released as APIs (C1 contract).

  • The CDS views no longer exist in the system.

  • When searching in ADT (Ctrl+Shift+A), the objects cannot be found.

  • In the "Manage API Snapshots" application, I cannot find these CDS views in the current SAP Default snapshot.

  • However, the compatibility ATC check still reports them as deleted APIs.

I found SAP KBA 3442779 which explains that released APIs may remain in historical API snapshots and can continue to trigger compatibility checks even after deletion.

My questions are:

  1. How can I identify the exact snapshot that still contains these deleted CDS views?

  2. If the objects no longer exist and were never delivered to customers, what is the recommended way to remove these compatibility findings?

  3. Is setting the default snapshot to "Not Check Relevant" the only solution, or is there another recommended approach?

  4. Has anyone experienced a similar issue with deleted released CDS views in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Warehouse Task Concept in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition – Mapping from SAP Business ByDesign Inte
entry.content:
We are currently working with a customer who uses a .NET-based solution integrated with SAP Business ByDesign.

In the current ByD setup, warehouse operators work based on logistics tasks generated by the system (e.g., Goods Receipt, Stock Transfer, Picking). Our WMS consumes these tasks and allows operators to execute and confirm them. This process is supported through APIs such as SiteLogisticsTask and WarehouseConfirmation.

 

The customer is now evaluating a transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and would like to maintain the same operational model.

 

While we understand that S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides Warehouse Management capabilities and supports API-based integration with external systems, we are struggling to identify the equivalent of the ByD logistics task concept.

 

Our questions are:

  • Is there an equivalent to ByD Logistics Tasks / Warehouse Tasks in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  • Can these tasks be created, retrieved, processed, and confirmed through standard APIs by an external WMS?
  • Which business objects and APIs should be considered as the equivalent of SiteLogisticsTask and WarehouseConfirmation in ByD?
  • Are there any limitations or recommended approaches when implementing a task-driven warehouse execution process with an external WMS in Public Cloud?

Our objective is to offer the customer the same warehouse execution experience they currently have in ByD while following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition best practices and Clean Core principles.

Any guidance, architecture recommendations, or references to relevant APIs and business objects would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Payload Corruption while posting pdf attachment via APIM.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I need to post attachments (PDF/TXT), from a 3rd party to S4 API "API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV" via APIM, 

While creating the request to fetch token the original payload in body is getting corrupted and not reaching to S4.

I have tried storing the payload in a variable and used that to post the request still the payload is not reaching S4.

API Management

entry.title: Is it possible to query Joule via an API?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I'm exploring the possibility of integrating SAP Joule with an external system.
My question is: does SAP currently expose an API to send queries/prompts to Joule programmatically and receive its responses, similar to how one would call a generative AI endpoint?
Specifically, I'd like to understand:

Is there an official API (REST or otherwise) to interact with Joule outside of its native UI?
If so, what licensing or SAP BTP setup is required to access it?
Are there any current limitations on which Joule capabilities (e.g., Joule for SAP S/4HANA vs. Joule Studio/custom skills) can be accessed this way?
Is this documented anywhere, or is it still in early access/roadmap stage?

Any guidance, documentation links, or experiences from those who've tried this would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

Joule Artificial Intelligence

entry.title: Can SAP API Management directly invoke SAP S/4HANA OData services without SAP Cloud Integration?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have an architectural question regarding the use of SAP API Management and would appreciate your insights.

Scenario

I have a client application that sends data to an API Proxy in SAP API Management. Instead of forwarding the request to an SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) iFlow, I am considering calling an SAP S/4HANA OData service directly from the API Proxy.

Since the target OData service requires an X-CSRF token for POST operations, the API Proxy would need to perform the following steps:

  1. Receive the incoming request from the client.
  2. Fetch the X-CSRF token from the SAP S/4HANA OData service.
  3. Reuse the token (and cookies, if required) to execute the POST request.
  4. Return the response back to the client.

My Questions

  1. Is this architecture technically feasible using only SAP API Management, without involving SAP Cloud Integration (CPI)?
  2. Can SAP API Management reliably handle the complete X-CSRF token flow (token retrieval, cookie handling, and POST request)?
  3. Is this approach recommended for production environments, or are there any technical limitations or best practices that should be considered?
  4. What would be the major drawbacks compared to using SAP Cloud Integration, particularly regarding:
    • Error handling
    • Retry mechanisms
    • Monitoring and logging
    • Security
    • Scalability
    • Maintainability

Why I'm Asking

The motivation behind this approach is to optimize SAP Cloud Integration message consumption and potentially reduce licensing costs for simple pass-through scenarios. If API Management can securely and reliably handle such integrations, it could eliminate the need for an iFlow in certain use cases.

Has anyone implemented a similar architecture in a production environment? If so, I would appreciate hearing about your experience and whether you would recommend this approach.

Thank you in advance for your guidance!

entry.title: Correlation of Different HTTP Methods in SAP Cloud ALM to ServiceNow Integration
entry.content:
We have integrated SAP Cloud ALM (CALM) with ServiceNow and have evaluated both the Table API and the Scripted API approaches.
Our requirement is to support the following operations:

POST – Create a new event/ticket in ServiceNow.
GET – Retrieve existing ticket details and correlation information.
PATCH – Update an existing ServiceNow ticket.

Current Challenge
When using the Scripted API, we are only able to configure and invoke a single HTTP method. As a result, we are unable to understand how multiple operations (POST, GET, and PATCH) can be handled within the same integration scenario.
For example:

When a Cloud ALM event is created, we need to perform a POST to create a ServiceNow ticket.
After the ticket is created, we need a GET to retrieve or validate the ServiceNow record and correlation details.
For subsequent incident updates, we need to perform a PATCH against the existing ServiceNow ticket.

However, in the Scripted API configuration, it appears that only one method call can be defined, which makes it unclear how create and update scenarios should be handled within the same integration flow.
Questions

  • How can SAP Cloud ALM invoke different HTTP methods (POST, GET, and PATCH) when using the Scripted API?
  • Is there a mechanism within Scripted API to dynamically switch the HTTP method based on the business scenario (create vs. update)?
  • How is the correlation between SAP Cloud ALM incidents and ServiceNow tickets maintained when different methods are required?

entry.title: Need API to read GL account details from public cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

I need an API to read GL account details from public cloud. The API API_GLACCOUNTINCHARTOFACCOUNTS_SRV is part of communication scenario SAP_COM_0087 which is not available in the system as the scope item 1YB is not active. Can anyone please let me know if there is any other API to read GL account details.

To activate scope item 1YB, do we need to have SAC license?

Thanks in advance

Poornima

entry.title: Testing SAP standard API on RISE backend
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

When testing SAP Standard OData APIs on SAP RISE, who in your team owns and maintains the Postman collection?

Who should provide the following? 

 1. OAuth 2.0 setup for IAS

 2. Environment variables for Dev/QA/Prod

 3. Example requests for all CRUD operations

It’s working well, but before we roll this out company-wide I’d like feedback:

1. Is there an SAP recommended way to package + version Postman collections?

2. Any security concerns sharing collections with tokens in them?

3. Does SAP plan to publish official Postman collections for all S/4HANA Cloud APIs?

4. Could you please guide me to setup postman collections?

Regards, 

Ramu.

entry.title: standad api in sap public cloud
entry.content:
hello all,

is there standard api i can use to integrate with third party want give him the data of (storage location - plant - materials in each plant - stock of this material in each plant ) need your help please ?

thanks in advance

entry.title: ACL for Products in SAP Integration Suite Developer Hub
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

Is it possible to define ACLs (Access Control Lists) for products in the Developer Hub? I have already read your guide regarding roles and role collections for the Developer Hub, but I could not find any information on how to set permissions specifically for products.

Could you please advise if this is supported, and if so, point me to the relevant documentation or steps?

Thank you in advance.
